phrases.intent_en: Ask a wine shop if they offer cellar tours
phrases.cultural_note: The Chartrons was the historic trading district of foreign wine merchants; several châis (wine warehouses) survive and some offer visits.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the vintage market takes place on weekends
phrases.text_local: Où se déroule le marché vintage le week-end dans ce quartier ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh day-ROOL luh mar-SHAY vin-TAZH luh WEE-kend dahn suh kar-TYAY
phrases.cultural_note: The Chartrons hosts a well-known vintage and brocante market on weekends along the quays.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the café serves local canelés
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que vous servez des canelés bordelais ?
phrases.pronunciation: es-kuh voo sair-VAY day kah-nuh-LAY bor-duh-LAY
phrases.cultural_note: Canelés are Bordeaux's iconic caramelised rum-and-vanilla pastries; every good café and bakery in the city should have them.

responses.english: Yes, of course, we have canelés.
responses.phonetic: bawn-ZHOOR, voo SERV duh KAHN-lay bor-duh-LAY
responses.french: Oui, bien sûr, nous avons des canelés.

phrases.intent_en: Ask a gallery owner about the artwork on display
phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous me parler de l'œuvre exposée ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-RYAY-voo muh par-LAY duh LUH-vruh ex-poh-ZAY ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: The Chartrons has evolved into a hub for contemporary art galleries alongside its antique dealers.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the nearest tram stop is
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve l'arrêt de tram le plus proche, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lah-RAY duh TRAM luh plyoo PROSH seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Tram line B runs through the Chartrons along the Garonne quays, making it easy to reach the city centre.

responses.english: Hello, the nearest tram stop is on the quays, line B.
responses.phonetic: OO suh TROOV la-RAY duh TRAHM luh PLEW PLEWS PROSH
responses.french: Bonjour, l'arrêt de tram le plus proche est sur les quais, ligne B.
